windows.embed_text_v2: swirling red dye into cream wax in a clear tumbler glass to make a marbled red-and-white candle, centered white wick, gloved hands on a white plate, dark apron behind
windows.summary_v2: Red dye is swirled into cream-colored wax in a clear tumbler, creating a marbled red-and-white pattern around a centered wick as gloved hands hold the plate.

tags_v2.action: swirling dye into wax to marble a candle

seconds_v2.desc: A clear tumbler on a white plate holds cream-colored wax with red dye starting to swirl through it; a white wick stands centered and a gloved hand steadies the plate against a dark apron background.
Red translucent dye continues marbling into the white wax, ribbons of red spreading down one side of the glass around the centered wick.
The red swirls deepen and stretch through the cream wax, creating a marbled blood-red-and-white pattern in the tumbler.
More red dye blooms at the top and threads downward, the marble effect growing more pronounced through the glass.
Vivid red veins now wind through the pale wax across the full height of the glass, wick still upright at center.
The red-and-cream marbling swirls further; a gloved hand visible at left holding the white plate steady on the dark backdrop.
Dense red feathered patterns swirl through the white wax, the marble design filling most of the tumbler.
Heavy red marbling now dominates the glass, swirling ribbons of red blending into the cream base as the colored candle takes shape.
